summary: Iteration 3: Tested brute-force lockout on /api/admin/login, Resend email graceful no-op, static catalog /catalog (list/filter/search/drawer/prefill-to-contact), home Browse-Catalog CTA, and admin regression. Backend: 11/11 pytest tests passed. Frontend: 10/10 Playwright flows passed. No bugs found.

design_issues.screen: Contact form prefill from catalog drawer
issues: Interest dropdown falls back to 'Not sure — advise me' whenever the catalog product name is not one of the six TECHS values (RFID / Barcode / Auto ID / RTLS / Label converting / Not sure). So clicking 'Request Quote' on, say, 'Zebra ZD230' correctly prefills the message 'I'd like a quote for: Zebra ZD230.' but shows 'Not sure — advise me' in the dropdown. Consider either (a) adding a 'Specific product (see message)' option, or (b) mapping catalog category → TECHS value (printer→Label converting, label/ribbon→Barcode) so the dropdown reflects intent better. Not a functional bug — message still carries the SKU.

action_items: (Optional UX) Improve catalog→contact interest-dropdown prefill so it doesn't fall back to 'Not sure — advise me' for specific SKUs. Suggested fix: in Contact.jsx, if prefill is not in TECHS, map by catalog category, or append a dedicated 'Specific product (see message)' option.
critical_code_review_comments: Brute-force lockout implementation is correct: identifier=IP:email, 5-fail/15-min window, Retry-After header set, successful login clears DB record, lock checked BEFORE password compare (prevents user enumeration timing leaks). Login-attempts collection has unique index on identifier.

Lockout logic resets attempts counter to 0 when locking (L218 of server.py) — this is intentional because locked_until enforces the wait; counter will start fresh after window expires. Verified behaviour in test_wrong_password_5_times_then_429 + test_correct_password_within_lockout_still_429.

send_lead_notification: graceful no-op verified both via direct unit call (returns None, no exception) and via live POST /api/inquiries (201 + backend log 'skipped (RESEND_API_KEY not set)'). Uses asyncio.to_thread so it won't block the event loop when keys ARE set later.
BackgroundTasks dispatch in create_inquiry means email latency never affects user-facing response — correct pattern.
Catalog is entirely client-side (16 SKUs in /app/frontend/src/data/catalog.js), no API calls; filter/search use useMemo; drawer uses local state — minimal surface area for bugs.

sessionStorage prefill key 'drishti_prefill_interest' is set by catalog drawer and consumed + cleared by Contact useEffect on mount. Verified clearing prevents accidental re-prefill on later navigations.
Lockout error message reveals attempts-related info in minutes; acceptable, but consider adding rate-limit to /api/inquiries too (currently unlimited public POST) as a future hardening item.
updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_lockout_email.py (new — 11 tests: lockout, email noop, admin regression)
seed_data_creation: Each run seeds 1 TEST_EmailNoop_* inquiry via public POST /api/inquiries. login_attempts collection is wiped before/after every test via autouse fixture so lockout state never leaks between runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Iteration 3 complete. New backend test file: /app/backend/tests/test_lockout_email.py — 3 lockout cases (5x 401 → 429 with Retry-After; correct-pw-during-lockout=429; successful-login-clears-attempts), 2 admin-login-works, 2 email-noop, 4 admin regression. Every test uses autouse fixture to wipe db.login_attempts so tests are fully isolated. Catalog at /catalog is client-side static (16 SKUs); filter/search/drawer/prefill-to-contact all verified via Playwright against REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL. Minor UX note only (interest dropdown fallback). No retest needed.
rca of the issue: No bugs. All iteration-3 features work as intended. RESEND_API_KEY and NOTIFICATION_EMAIL are empty in /app/backend/.env — this is the intentional no-op production state (user will add keys later); email_service.py logs 'skipped (RESEND_API_KEY not set)' and returns None without raising.
